activities_projects.title: Theme Identification
activities_projects.objective: Understand the main theme of the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the two possible ways the world may end
Write two sentences explaining the theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the central idea of the poem

activities_projects.title: Meaning of Fire
activities_projects.objective: Understand the symbolic meaning of fire
steps: Read the line mentioning fire
Find what emotion fire represents
Write 2–3 sentences explaining it

activities_projects.title: Meaning of Ice
activities_projects.objective: Understand the symbolic meaning of ice
steps: Read the line mentioning ice
Identify the emotion connected with ice
Write 2–3 sentences explaining the idea

activities_projects.title: Poet’s Opinion
steps: Read the poem again
Identify which idea the poet supports first
Write one or two lines explaining why

activities_projects.title: Rhyme Scheme Activity
activities_projects.objective: Identify rhyme scheme in the poem
steps: Read the poem aloud
Underline rhyming words
Write the rhyme scheme of the poem

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Builder
steps: Select three difficult words from the poem
Write their meanings
Use each word in a sentence

activities_projects.title: Short Summary Writing
steps: Read the poem carefully
Write a 3–4 sentence summary of the poem

activities_projects.title: Message of the Poem
steps: Think about the emotions mentioned in the poem
Write two sentences explaining the message of the poem

activities_projects.title: Creative Drawing
steps: Draw two pictures representing fire and ice
Label each drawing with the emotion it represents

activities_projects.title: Personal Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Relate the poem to real life
steps: Think about how anger or hatred affects people
Write 3 sentences on how emotions can cause harm
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates poem ideas to real life
